author: lOrm
content:
Refactored — schemas now live on each ToolHandler via .

Forensic evidence:
- Pre-refactor get_tool_definitions(): 684-line json! macro at src/tools/mod.rs:112-789 with #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] (line 111).
- Post-refactor: src/tools/mod.rs:117-119 — get_tool_definitions returns Value::Array(registry::iter_handlers().map(ToolHandler::definition).collect()). #[allow] removed.
- Each handler in src/tools/registry.rs now has fn definition(&self) -> Value next to fn execute, eliminating the drift surface called out in the issue.
- Byte-for-byte round-trip verified: serde_json::to_string(get_tool_definitions()) SHA256 = 765e6c391709fcfe000c2105516b90c4e1707bc49bb16f82a0b340bb00c5b066 — identical before & after refactor.
- Two new regression tests pin the invariant:
  * handler_definition_name_matches_handler_name — every handler.definition().function.name == handler.name().
  * get_tool_definitions_matches_handler_registry_order — array order mirrors registry::HANDLERS so handler addition can't desync the model-facing list.
- Latent bug from issue point #4 (cron_delete schema declares no required fields but execute_cron_delete requires one-of {id,name}) is now flagged with a comment next to the handler instead of buried in a stub schema. JSON schema oneOf fix tracked as follow-up.
- Two previously-orphan schemas (list_mcp_resources, read_mcp_resource — schemas exposed to the model, no handler registered) now have stub handlers that emit a clear 'dispatch not yet wired' error, closing the schema-vs-code gap on that pair too.
- LOC: mod.rs 2506→1867 (-639); registry.rs 473→1307 (+834); net +195 (schemas are duplicated structurally as one-per-handler json! blocks instead of one giant literal, but each is local to its handler — the structural-modularity gain the issue called for).

cargo build, cargo test (1360 lib + 131 integration), and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all exit 0. No #[allow], no clippy.toml, no #[ignore] added.
